rationale: The 2022 paper cites Van Heerde, Leeflang, and Wittink (2000) only once as an example of a possible postpromotion dip when interpreting what a long-term elasticity captures. The cited work is not used to build hypotheses, define core constructs, or provide the main methodological foundation of the two-stage cross-national elasticity model. Its role is a brief contextual illustration within a broader discussion of dynamic effects.
